gap: Reserved names are proved, but the concrete lexer text boundary is still represented by token models and tests.
Dynamic names have environment roundtrips, but the production parser still needs a direct CF connection.

Token-list parser simulation, raw char-list and mlstring lexer/parser models, public parse-from-string result-shape wrappers, and small CF wrappers are proved; the remaining gap is composing the translated pure parser with the top-level runner/string boundary rather than proving a second handwritten recursive parser body.

No-explicit-fuel source-string public parser entrypoints choose fuel from the source length and have translated/app-spec/CF wrapper evidence; lexer no-fuel bound, parser rest-decrease, doubled public token bound, atom/command/program token no-fuel, and shipped atom/command/program no-parser-fuel are proved; the remaining gap is whole-runner composition of these translated parser entrypoints with the exact top-level string/eval pipeline.

Prototype Sym-string token/command/program parser model is proved related to the source-string parser, including public bounded no-explicit-fuel entrypoints, proto atom/command/program no-fuel guarantees, a shipped-body no-parser-fuel theorem for the factored command shape, and shipped-style no-parser-fuel string-error result wrappers with translated/app-spec/CF wrapper evidence; the remaining gap is top-level composition, not a second handwritten recursive parser proof.
The exact raw nested marked return block is still not the main proven artifact; the factored refactor target is proved.
Already-evaluated primitive value wrappers are proved; full recursive shipped evaluator branch CF proof is not complete.
Substitution and matcher soundness are model-level; efficient executable matcher bridge coverage is still slice-based.
The match fragment has translated/generated slice evidence, but the whole runner match path is not yet proved.
Pure chain payload helpers are certified; the complete handwritten chain evaluator wrapper still needs a direct proof.
Case/switch payload wrappers are proved; switch now evaluates its scrutinee before branch selection to match CeTTa/HE; whole recursive branch CF proof remains narrower than the model theorem.
Let-star semantic/model proof is strong, but executable wrapper verification is still payload-level.
Typed/evalc fragment wrappers are proved for selected shapes; named-space context and dependent type behavior are not fully integrated.
This proves the HOL model payload partition, not the whole shipped executable or parser/runner/FFI boundary.

There is now a HOL runner-kernel slice over imported numeric commands plus a translated/app-spec/CF factored state-effect helper for bind!/new-space and add-atom cases, and a source-string-to-runner semantic composition through the public bound parser and source-command import layer; the remaining gap is still the exact shipped top-level runner wrapper, its CF proof, and fuller module/import filesystem behavior.
Finite call profiles are proved and the FFI probe runs; real external-call semantics and effects are not yet verified.
Oracle comparisons anchor behavior against CeTTa/HE, but they are differential tests and not semantic proofs.
